all_labels.explanation: Preference-Accumulate: Acknowledging conciseness/revision preference sets constraints that indirectly shape how the 3-day plan will be delivered.
Option-Select: Choosing Korean and conciseness determines presentation of the trip plan, supporting delivery but not itself producing the plan content.
Intent-Reveal: Explicitly evaluates/claims that the 3-day Pittsburgh plan requirement has been completed.
No substantive effect on creating, modifying, or fulfilling the 3-day trip plan requirement; it is a generic follow-up invitation.
No substantive effect on the requirement; it is a closing/send-off rather than plan production or revision.
Produces the revised Day 2 plan explicitly focused on downtown outdoor spots and removing the Warhol Museum, fulfilling r10’s replacement.
Keeps the morning Strip District portion, which supports the overall Day 2 structure but does not itself enforce the post-lunch downtown switch.
Keeps the lunch anchor at Wholey’s, directly maintaining the 'after lunch' reference point needed for r10 to apply.
Adds a downtown outdoor stop (Point State Park) as the post-lunch destination, operationalizing 'go downtown after lunch.'
Suggests Market Square as a downtown outdoor option to replace the Warhol Museum after lunch, aligning with r10.
Suggests PPG Plaza as another downtown outdoor stop, further executing the requirement to go downtown after lunch instead of Warhol.
Schedules Mount Washington later in the day; it’s consistent with the revised downtown-first sequence but not required by r10.
Adds a dinner plan; it complements the overall Day 2 downtown-focused itinerary but does not itself satisfy the post-lunch downtown requirement.
English study examples are unrelated to enforcing or fulfilling the post-lunch downtown routing requirement.
Feedback-Adopt: the revised Day 2 summary explicitly reorients downtown time toward outdoor spots (fulfilling r11).
Preference-Accumulate: keeping the Strip District walk preserves the overall itinerary structure but does not itself enforce 'downtown outdoor' prioritization.
Preference-Accumulate: keeping Wholey's lunch maintains user preferences; it is adjacent context rather than downtown-outdoor prioritization work.
Option-Select: proposing Point State Park directly adds a downtown outdoor activity, supporting r11.
Option-Select: suggesting Market Square (outdoor downtown browsing/people-watching) directly satisfies the outdoor-downtown prioritization.
Option-Select: suggesting PPG Plaza as an outdoor downtown stop directly advances r11.
Mount Washington via the Duquesne Incline is not part of 'downtown time' prioritization; it is a separate segment of Day 2.
Preference-Accumulate: scheduling an indoor dinner downtown affects how downtown time is allocated (and could dilute outdoor prioritization) without explicitly addressing r11.
English study expressions are unrelated to the downtown-outdoor prioritization requirement.

Gives general selection strategy (lean/mild/seafood/veg) that guides compliant ordering but is not a specific item list.

Directly recommends Pollo a la Parrilla as a non-gamey option, satisfying the constraint.
Directly recommends Camarones (shrimp) to avoid gamey meat, fulfilling the requirement.
Directly recommends fish as a lighter, non-gamey choice at Gaucho, fulfilling the requirement.
Recommends non-meat sides (corn/vegetables) that inherently comply with the no-gamey-meat constraint.
Suggests sauces to manage flavor intensity; supports the constraint indirectly but doesn’t itself ensure non-gamey meat selection.
Explicitly warns what to avoid (mixed grills/offal/strong sausage) to stay within the no-gamey-meat constraint.
